headline: Bond investors propose crisis 'pause clauses' for emerging countries
summary: JOHANNESBURG, April 20 (Reuters) - Major bond investors including Amundi and T. Rowe Price have proposed adding clauses to sovereign bonds that would allow emerging countries to pause ‌debt payments for up to a year without defaulting in the event of a ‌crisis.
